perturbation.original_text: 3. DISTRIBUTION OF ARGO'S PRODUCTS BY YEC As part of the implementation and performance of this Agreement, both parties are entering into the DA concurrently with this Agreement. Pursuant to the DA, and subject to its terms, ARGO agreed to appoint YEC as the exclusive distributor of its products in the Territory specified therein, and YEC agreed to market and distribute Argo's products in a professional manner. In case the DA is terminated in accordance with the terms and conditions of the DA, this provision shall be considered void.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. DISTRIBUTION OF ARGO'S PRODUCTS BY YEC As part of the implementation and performance of this Agreement, both parties are entering into the DA concurrently with this Agreement. Pursuant to the DA, and subject to its terms, ARGO agreed to appoint YEC as the exclusive reseller of its products in the Territory specified therein, and YEC agreed to market and distribute Argo's products in a professional manner. In case the DA is terminated in accordance with the terms and conditions of the DA, this provision shall be considered void.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'distributor' is changed to 'reseller'. This introduces ambiguity because the legal implications of being a 'distributor' versus a 'reseller' are different, especially concerning rights and obligations related to product warranty, liability, and market exclusivity. While the Distribution Agreement is referenced, altering the term in the main agreement creates a conflict as to the intended scope and power of YEC's role. This contradiction occurs in the same location where the term was perturbed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. DISTRIBUTION OF ARGO'S PRODUCTS BY YEC As part of the implementation and performance of this Agreement, both parties are entering into the DA concurrently with this Agreement. Pursuant to the DA, and subject to its terms, ARGO agreed to appoint YEC as the exclusive reseller of its products in the Territory specified therein, and YEC agreed to market and distribute Argo's products in a professional manner. In case the DA is terminated in accordance with the terms and conditions of the DA, this provision shall be considered void.
perturbation.justification: While the explanation details a potential ambiguity arising from the change of 'distributor' to 'reseller', the contradicted text is identical to the changed text. Therefore, there isn't a direct, strong contradiction created within the document itself based on the provided excerpts. The potential legal difference between the terms might lead to disputes, but the document itself isn't internally contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: 4. JOINT STEERING COMMITTEE YEC and ARGO agree to pursue further opportunities for collaboration in the areas of research and development, manufacturing, marketing and sales, for the purposes stated in this Agreement. As a platform to discuss such opportunities, YEC and ARGO agree to form a Joint Steering Committee (hereafter "JSC"), which will meet at least four (4) times per year, once in every quarter, for the following purposes: (a) Subject to the DA being in effect, to review and share the progress of marketing and sales of ARGO Products by YEC and ARGOworldwide, as defined in the DA. (b) Subject to the DA being in effect, to establish sales targets and minimum purchase requirements for ARGO's Products under thedistribution relationship that is defined in the DA. (c) To discuss the possibilities of sales of YEC's products in the healthcare field using ARGO's sales network. (d) To discuss possibilities for improvements of ARGO's Products, especially improvements necessary for YEC to successfully market ARGO's products as defined in the DA, by granting YEC access to cost information and applying YEC's know-how and expertise in the field of motion control and robotics. (e) To discuss the responsibilities and areas of coverage of YEC and ARGO in the future research and development of ARGO Products, and to establish rules for proper compensation of the developing party for usage and/or licensing of any invention, know-how and improvement created by such party in the course of joint development. (f) To discuss the potential for licensed manufacturing of ARGO's Products by YEC. (g) To discuss the potential for quality improvements of ARGO's Products by applying YEC's know-how and expertise in the field ofmotion control and robotics.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. JOINT STEERING COMMITTEE YEC and ARGO agree to pursue further opportunities for collaboration in the areas of research and development, manufacturing and merchandising, marketing and sales, for the purposes stated in this Agreement. As a platform to discuss such opportunities, YEC and ARGO agree to form a Joint Steering Committee (hereafter "JSC"), which will meet at least four (4) times per year, once in every quarter, for the following purposes: (a) Subject to the DA being in effect, to review and share the progress of marketing and sales of ARGO Products by YEC and ARGOworldwide, as defined in the DA. (b) Subject to the DA being in effect, to establish sales targets and minimum purchase requirements for ARGO's Products under thedistribution relationship that is defined in the DA. (c) To discuss the possibilities of sales of YEC's products in the healthcare field using ARGO's sales network. (d) To discuss possibilities for improvements of ARGO's Products, especially improvements necessary for YEC to successfully market ARGO's products as defined in the DA, by granting YEC access to cost information and applying YEC's know-how and expertise in the field of motion control and robotics. (e) To discuss the responsibilities and areas of coverage of YEC and ARGO in the future research and development of ARGO Products, and to establish rules for proper compensation of the developing party for usage and/or licensing of any invention, know-how and improvement created by such party in the course of joint development. (f) To discuss the potential for licensed manufacturing of ARGO's Products by YEC. (g) To discuss the potential for quality improvements of ARGO's Products by applying YEC's know-how and expertise in the field ofmotion control and robotics.
perturbation.explanation: Inserting the term 'merchandising' alongside 'manufacturing' creates an internal inconsistency. While manufacturing refers to the physical production of ARGO's products, merchandising involves the display and promotion of those products for retail. Since these two have completely different understanding the Joint Steering Committe has conflicting purposes. It's unclear whether the Joint Steering Committee is intended to discuss only manufacturing aspects or also the merchandising aspects, leading to potential disputes over the committee's scope. This perturbation is in section 4 and creates a contradiction within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. JOINT STEERING COMMITTEE YEC and ARGO agree to pursue further opportunities for collaboration in the areas of research and development, manufacturing and merchandising, marketing and sales, for the purposes stated in this Agreement. As a platform to discuss such opportunities, YEC and ARGO agree to form a Joint Steering Committee (hereafter "JSC"), which will meet at least four (4) times per year, once in every quarter, for the following purposes: (a) Subject to the DA being in effect, to review and share the progress of marketing and sales of ARGO Products by YEC and ARGOworldwide, as defined in the DA. (b) Subject to the DA being in effect, to establish sales targets and minimum purchase requirements for ARGO's Products under thedistribution relationship that is defined in the DA. (c) To discuss the possibilities of sales of YEC's products in the healthcare field using ARGO's sales network. (d) To discuss possibilities for improvements of ARGO's Products, especially improvements necessary for YEC to successfully market ARGO's products as defined in the DA, by granting YEC access to cost information and applying YEC's know-how and expertise in the field of motion control and robotics. (e) To discuss the responsibilities and areas of coverage of YEC and ARGO in the future research and development of ARGO Products, and to establish rules for proper compensation of the developing party for usage and/or licensing of any invention, know-how and improvement created by such party in the course of joint development. (f) To discuss the potential for licensed manufacturing of ARGO's Products by YEC. (g) To discuss the potential for quality improvements of ARGO's Products by applying YEC's know-how and expertise in the field ofmotion control and robotics.
perturbation.justification: The insertion of 'merchandising' alongside 'manufacturing' alters the scope of the Joint Steering Committee's responsibilities. The original text focused on manufacturing-related activities. The changed text adds merchandising, potentially leading to discussions and decisions outside the initial scope, creating an inconsistency about the intended focus of the committee's efforts as stated in section 4. This misaligned terminology thus introduces a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Property"). Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Properties supplied pursuant to this Agreement. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Properties are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Properties, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, the parties will negotiate, in good faith, in an attempt to conclude one or more appropriate license agreements prior to either party's use of the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Assets"). Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Assets supplied pursuant to this Agreement. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Assets are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Assets, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, the parties will negotiate, in good faith, in an attempt to conclude one or more appropriate license agreements prior to either party's use of the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the collective term from 'Intellectual Property' to 'Intellectual Assets' creates confusion about what the agreement covers. While both terms relate to intangible assets, 'Intellectual Property' typically has a more defined legal meaning, encompassing specific protected rights. By switching to 'Intellectual Assets', the agreement introduces ambiguity, making it unclear whether it includes all forms of intellectual assets or only those traditionally considered 'Intellectual Property'. This contradiction will create disputes during joint development mentioned at the end of the section because one place mentions about Intellectual Property and the start of the section uses Intellectual Assets. This perturbation is in section 8, but the contradiction is generated in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY; CO-DEVELOPMENT, IMPROVEMENT, MODIFICATION This agreement does not give either party any rights, title or interest in the other party's trade name, trademarks, copyrights, patents, trade secrets, know-how, proprietary data, confidential information, or other intellectual property (hereinafter collectively "Intellectual Assets"). Except as expressly stipulated in this Agreement, each party shall not without any prior written consent, use, copy, modify or license the other party's Intellectual Assets supplied pursuant to this Agreement. Unless otherwise agreed between the parties or stipulated in a separate related agreement such as the SPA or DA, each party confirms and agrees that any Intellectual Assets are hereby supplied to the other party on an "as is" basis. There are no warranties by either party with respect to such Intellectual Assets, express or implied including the implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In the event that the parties mutually agree to explore jointly in any manner, design and/or develop new products or improve or modify ARGO's current products, the parties will negotiate, in good faith, in an attempt to conclude one or more appropriate license agreements prior to either party's use of the Intellectual Property of the other.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Intellectual Property' to 'Intellectual Assets' in the beginning of section 8 while retaining 'Intellectual Property' at the end of the same section creates a contradiction. The agreement now uses two different terms to refer to potentially the same concept, leading to ambiguity about which types of assets are covered under the licensing agreement, especially during joint development activities. This constitutes a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations within the same section (section 8).
